mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production directly maps to higher corporate output and earnings capacity, causing the S&P 500 to generate positive 5-day forward returns as equity prices adjust to stronger real activity data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are positive in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1.5 million units signal acute weakness in construction and durable goods demand, which propagates through supplier networks and reduces S&P 500 earnings expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances (HOUST < 1500) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in retail sales and housing starts capture a broad collapse in consumer and residential demand that is stronger than either series alone, amplifying recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, 60-day forward returns are not significantly more negative than when only one declines (t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, sustained payroll employment growth signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve into additional overtightening, raising terminal rate fears and generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: During late-cycle regimes, positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the Rank IC is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increases corporate borrowing costs, mechanically reducing present values and producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in the highest FEDFUNDS quartile are not significantly lower than the lowest quartile (t-test p > 0.05), or the Rank IC is above -0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4 percent, restrictive monetary policy actively contracts liquidity and credit availability, causing immediate risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or the triggered signal Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales falling below 700 billion dollars indicates a sharp contraction in consumer spending that directly reduces top-line revenue for a broad swath of S&P 500 constituents, leading to negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances (RSAFS < 700000), or the Rank IC is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in industrial production and a high federal funds rate reveal a policy-induced growth slowdown where restrictive monetary policy bites into real output, a combination that equity markets reprice downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and FEDFUNDS > 3.5% does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of months, or the interaction term Rank IC is above -0.03.
